title: 10 best herbal teas for over bleeding during periods

remedy_properties: Astringent compounds: The tea's astringent compounds, such as tannins and flavonoids, help to reduce blood loss by contracting the uterine muscles and constricting the blood vessels, thereby slowing down bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Guelder Rose tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and spasms that may contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Antioxidant activity: The tea's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and flavonoids, help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive bleeding, promoting overall uterine health and reducing menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Viburnum opulus, also known as guelder rose, teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich content of organic compounds and bioactive molecules. The tea has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramps and ease heavy bleeding. The plant's tannins and flavonoids also have a soothing effect on the uterine walls, promoting a healthy balance of blood flow and reducing excessive bleeding. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins in Viburnum opulus help to reduce bleeding by contracting the blood vessels and promoting clotting, thereby reducing menstrual flow.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid in Viburnum opulus has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Flavonoids, such as quercetin, in Viburnum opulus have a uterotonic effect, meaning they help to tone and strengthen the uterine muscles, which can help to reduce menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Viburnum opulus are used to make teas as they are believed to have astringent properties that help reduce bleeding.
Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum opulus are used to make teas as they are rich in antioxidants and are thought to help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding.
Barks: The barks of Viburnum opulus are used to make teas as they contain tannins that help to reduce bleeding and soothe the uterus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of viburnum opulus leaves and flowers in the morning when they are completely dry.
Rinse the harvested plant material with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the plant material in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool completely before serving or storing it in the refrigerator.

remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover tea contains isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce blood flow and ease menopausal symptoms, including heavy bleeding during periods. This is because isoflavones work as natural estrogen receptors in the body, regulating hormonal imbalances that can contribute to heavy bleeding.

Vitamin K: Red clover tea is a rich source of vitamin K, which plays a crucial role in blood clotting and platelet function. Adequate vitamin K levels can help reduce menstrual bleeding by promoting healthy coagulation and preventing excessive bleeding.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in red clover tea, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy bleeding during periods.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense, also known as red clover, teas helps with over bleeding during periods because it's rich in isoflavones, specifically formononetin and genistein, which mimic estrogen in the body. These compounds help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to heavy bleeding. Additionally, red clover teas have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote uterine health, thus alleviating symptoms associated with heavy menstrual bleeding. Its natural properties also help to balance the body's natural rhythms, promoting a more regular menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly genistein and formononetin, help to alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding by exerting an estrogen-like effect, which can help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce uterine contractions.
Coumarins: Coumarins, specifically coumarin and scopoletin, have a vasoconstrictive effect, which can help reduce blood loss by constricting blood vessels and improving blood clotting.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus, thereby reducing heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Trifolium pratense are used to make teas for over bleeding during periods because they contain isoflavones that help to balance estrogen levels and reduce menstrual bleeding.

Flowers: The flowers of Trifolium pratense are used to make teas for over bleeding during periods because they are rich in isoflavones, particularly formononetin and genistein, which help to reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of PMS.

Roots: The roots of Trifolium pratense are used to make teas for over bleeding during periods because they contain triterpenoid saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh trifolium pratense flowers or 10 grams of dried flowers.
Rinse the flowers with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep the trifolium pratense flowers in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine mesh sieve into a separate container to remove the flowers.
Store the herbal tea in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Chamomile tea helps to constrict blood vessels, which can reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation by reducing blood flow to the uterus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: A flavonoid found in Matricaria chamomilla, which helps with over bleeding during periods by exerting a mild antispasmodic effect, reducing uterine contractions and thereby alleviating excessive menstrual flow.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are commonly used to make teas due to their high concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has a soothing effect on the uterine muscles and helps to reduce bleeding.
Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make teas that can help to reduce menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms of over bleeding due to their ability to regulate estrogen levels.

Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make teas that can help to reduce menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms of over bleeding due to their ability to reduce inflammation and regulate hormone balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Rinse the flowers gently with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Use a mortar and pestle to gently crush the flowers into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried or 2 teaspoons of the fresh chamomilla flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and let it cool to room temperature.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and kaempferol, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate heavy bleeding.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Cut the harvested roots into smaller pieces weighing about 5-10 grams per serving.
Combine the cut roots with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup or teapot.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding, allowing for easier healing and reduced symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Turmeric tea's antioxidant properties help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by excessive bleeding, reducing the risk of anemia and promoting overall health during this time.

Blood clotting regulation: The active compounds in turmeric tea, particularly curcuminoids, have been shown to regulate blood clotting and platelet activity, helping to reduce excessive bleeding and promote normal menstrual flow.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, teas helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric tea has been shown to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy bleeding. Additionally, curcumin helps to improve blood clotting and reduce inflammation in the uterine lining, thereby reducing excessive menstrual flow and promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Roots: Roots are also commonly used for teas as they contain a high amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce bleeding.
Leaves: Leaves are sometimes used for teas, particularly in traditional medicine, as they are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 turmeric roots 6-8 inches long and 1-2 inches wide from mature plants in the morning.
Clean the turmeric roots under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Slice the turmeric roots into thin pieces about 1/8 inch thick and 1-2 inches long.
Steep 1 teaspoon of sliced turmeric in 8 oz of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup and discard the solids immediately.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees during fall season.

Steep 1 teaspoon of the powder in 250 milliliters of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.

Combine 1 teaspoon of ground cinnamomum verum powder with 1 cup of boiling water in a tea infuser.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in late summer for optimal potency and effectiveness.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal teas are digestive issues such as diarrhea, constipation, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger can help soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms. Additionally, herbal teas like dandelion root and turmeric may be used to treat menorrhagia-like bleeding in the stomach or intestines due to conditions such as hemorrhoids or ulcers.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for over bleeding during periods are natural remedies that help alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding by regulating hormonal balances and promoting uterine health. These teas are rich in essential nutrients and antioxidants that address underlying causes of heavy periods. The best herbal teas for over bleeding during periods include Vitex agnus-castus, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Trifolium pratense, Cimicifuga racemosa, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica sinensis, Crataegus monogyna, Paeonia lactiflora, Althaea officinalis, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, heavy periods can lead to fatigue, anemia, and decreased productivity, significantly impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with over bleeding during periods is a blend of Raspberry Leaf, Red Clover, and Dandelion Root teas. Raspberry Leaf tea helps to tone the uterus and reduce bleeding, while Red Clover tea provides essential minerals and reduces inflammation. Dandelion Root tea aids in digestion and reduces menstrual cramps. Drinking a combination of these teas, particularly during the first few days of your period, can help to regulate and reduce excessive bleeding.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current drug discovery technologies, herbal teas like Fomentex can be beneficial in reducing menstrual bleeding irregularities. The study involved 40 women with oligomenorrhea and secondary amenorrhea, who were divided into two groups: one receiving Fomentex herbal tea (Foeniculum vulgare / Mentha longifolia / Vitex agnus-castus) and the other receiving medroxy progesterone acetate (MP). The results showed that while the number of women experiencing bleeding during the first cycle was higher in the MP group (94.1%), the percentage of patients with on-time menstruation in the next (drug-free) episode was higher in the Fomentex group compared with the MP group (50% vs. 23.5%), indicating a potential benefit of Fomentex in inducing regular bleeding.
